title: senecio eremophilus subsp. kingii
intro: Senecio eremophilus subsp. kingii, also known as King's Ragwort, is a plant species that belongs to the daisy family (Asteraceae). It is native to Western Australia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to act as a diuretic and to reduce inflammation. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs, and supporting the treatment of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. Senecio eremophilus subsp. kingii is often used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be cautious due to potential toxicity and side effects.
